page: Microsoft Azure E128s_v7 is an Esv7-family memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with two threads per core. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes a substantial 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix operations, and hardware-assisted cryptography (AES, VAES, and SHA_NI). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the E128s_v7 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, though its single-core capabilities remain in the average tier. Multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores both rank in the top 10% of tested instances, reflecting the high parallel processing capacity of its 128 vCPUs. The instance also excels in data processing tasks, achieving top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression (36.22 GB/sec) and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (704.78 GB/sec). Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching a top-tier 189.85 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring in the top 25%. For large language model inference, the instance delivers top-tier prompt processing speeds for 70B models (45.31 tokens/sec) and strong performance for 7B models, though text generation speeds for larger models fall into the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E128s_v7 offers high resource density for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces baseline hardware overhead, making it a cost-effective choice for memory-bound applications relying on external premium storage. Given its massive 1024.0 GB memory capacity, high-throughput parallel processing, and advanced instruction support, this instance is well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise-grade data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E128s_v7 is an Esv7-family memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides 8.0 GB of memory per core and a 480 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While single-core performance is average, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high-speed data decompression, and exceptional Redis SET throughput of 189.85 million operations per second. It lacks local storage and GPUs but is premium storage capable, offering high resource density for memory-intensive workloads. The E128s_v7 is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, heavy data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E128s_v7 is an Esv7-family memory-optimized VM with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core CPU performance.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual machine delivering exceptional multi-core processing and massive memory capacity for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of RAM on Intel Xeon architecture
Top-tier multi-core CPU and data decompression performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix operations
High-throughput Redis performance reaching 189.85 million operations per second
Premium storage capable configuration without local disk overhead
Best suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and data processing
